verdict.note: Scratch repo: .spec/.forum with committed threads (one w/ reply history), no .issues. First store touch (spex issues --all) renamed the store: .forum gone, .issues holds all threads, exactly ONE 'issues: store dir .spec/.forum → .spec/.issues' commit recorded as a git rename (R100), git log --follow traces through the rename into full pre-rename history, replies parse identically, and the triggering read returned the full store. Also verified on a clone of the LIVE repo (32 real threads → migrated, --follow on a real thread traced create→reply→resolve→rename).
Fresh repo, neither dir: spex propose created .spec/.issues and committed the thread there; no migration ran; .spec/.forum never created.
Pathological repo with BOTH .forum and .issues: any store touch fails LOUD naming both dirs + the manual repair (reconcile/remove/re-run); both dirs left intact, no silent merge.

Burst of 6 concurrent spex propose as the first touch after update: exactly ONE rename commit (store lock serializes find-check-move; racers re-check under the lock), seeded thread survived, all 6 concurrent writes landed under .issues.

throwaway clone: 2 threads (one with 2-reply history) in legacy .spec/.forum; first touch = spex issues --all. Exactly one 'issues: store dir' commit, R100 git-detected rename, --follow traces into pre-rename history, before/after --all --json byte-identical, triggering read returned the full store.
fresh repo, neither dir: first 'spex issues open' created .spec/.issues and committed the thread; .spec/.forum never appeared.
repo with BOTH dirs: read touch AND write touch exit 1 with an error naming both dirs + the manual repair (reconcile into .issues, git rm .forum, re-run); neither dir mutated, no silent merge/pick.

legacy .forum + 6 concurrent 'spex issues open' as the very first touch: exactly ONE rename commit, both migrated threads intact, all 6 concurrent writes committed under .spec/.issues — store lock serialized find-check-move.
